data.title: Hold Rating Reiterated as Outlook De-Risked but Operational Pressures Persist; $34 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Max Rakhlenko maintained a Hold rating on Tractor Supply yesterday and set a price target of $34.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Max Rakhlenko has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including a de-risked outlook but lingering operational pressures. He views the updated FY26 guidance as more achievable, with easier comparisons and early signs of improvement in the Companion Animal business, yet he still sees ongoing pressure from weaker ticket trends, particularly in big-ticket categories and lower units per transaction.

At the same time, he believes the removal of the long-term algorithm has eliminated a key overhang and made the stock more investable, but expects shares to trade largely within a range until sales trends and margins show more durable improvement. He remains cautious on gross margin, citing persistent pricing and transportation headwinds as tariff benefits fade, and therefore keeps a balanced stance with an unchanged price target of $34, reflecting modest upside but not enough conviction for a more positive rating.

According to TipRanks, Rakhlenko is a 3-star analyst with an average return of 3.3% and a 52.97% success rate. Rakhlenko covers the Consumer Cyclical sector, focusing on stocks such as Planet Fitness, Boot Barn, and AutoZone.

In another report released on July 20, Piper Sandler also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $32.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: SAP SE: Buy Rating Reiterated as Robust Cloud Momentum Supports Unchanged $210 Price Target
data.description:
Analyst Charles Brennan from Jefferies maintained a Buy rating on SAP SE and keeping the price target at €210.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Charles Brennan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, primarily the stronger-than-anticipated performance versus consensus expectations. He had projected that SAP’s cloud and commercial business (CCB) would surpass the already demanding 24% growth bar, and the actual 26% outcome signals a very robust second-quarter order intake and underlying demand.

Despite investor concerns around softer margin trends in the second quarter, Brennan views the solid order momentum as a key indicator of sustainable top-line expansion going forward. Against a backdrop of cautious market sentiment, he believes this strength in orders and cloud traction justifies maintaining a constructive stance on the shares, supporting his unchanged price target of $210 and his Buy recommendation on SAP SE.

Brennan covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Computacenter, Temenos, and Sage Group plc. According to TipRanks, Brennan has an average return of 8.7% and a 58.12% success rate on recommended stocks.

data.title: Analyst Maintains Buy on T-Mobile, Slightly Trims Price Target to $260 Amid Strong Profitability and FCF Outlook
data.description:
Gregory Williams, an analyst from TD Cowen, maintained the Buy rating on T Mobile US. The associated price target was lowered to $260.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Gregory Williams has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including robust profitability and cash generation despite softer reported revenue. He emphasizes that T-Mobile is still delivering upside in EBITDA and free cash flow, reaffirming its 2026 EBITDA outlook and improving FCF guidance, while continuing to post strong phone churn and account metrics that underscore healthy underlying demand.

Williams acknowledges the more cautious 3Q EBITDA guide, seeing it as reflecting near-term competitive and execution risks rather than a structural deterioration in the story. He argues that T-Mobile’s differentiated network, pricing flexibility, broadband initiatives, and disciplined capacity deployment in FWA position the company to grow faster than the broader industry, supporting a Buy rating even as he trims his price target slightly to $260.

In another report released yesterday, Goldman Sachs also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $230.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on AppFolio as Modestly Raised 2026 Guidance and Premium Valuation Reflect Sustained High-Teens Growth and Margin Expansion
data.description:
William Blair analyst Stephen Sheldon has reiterated their bullish stance on APPF stock, giving a Buy rating on July 22.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Stephen Sheldon has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight AppFolio’s strong fundamental performance and outlook. The company modestly raised its 2026 revenue and profitability guidance, supported by increased adoption of premium tiers, new business units, and additional solutions such as Realm-X Performers and resident services, signaling confidence in sustained high-teens revenue growth and expanding operating margins.

He also notes that, while AppFolio’s valuation multiples sit slightly above those of other vertical SaaS peers, the premium is warranted by faster growth, consistent unit expansion, and rising attach rates of value-added services. Sheldon expects the company to leverage its scale to deliver ongoing margin improvement and believes enhanced communication and transparency with investors will further support the stock, justifying a Buy recommendation despite the elevated multiples.

In another report released on July 22, Guggenheim also initiated coverage with a Buy rating on the stock with a $232.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
